arguments.task: Visit Reducto official website and official first-party pages only. Find and extract evidence for: product description, developer/API documentation, authentication methods, pricing/access/self-serve status, API surface type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None), rate limits, changelog/release notes if any, official GitHub or MCP server/repository if any. Prefer pages on reducto.ai and official GitHub only. Return the exact URLs visited and concise findings with quotes/snippets where possible.

input.thought: Start a focused browser research run on official Reducto sources.

raw_result.description: Document ingestion and parsing API for turning complex PDFs, images, and spreadsheets into structured data for AI workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs indicate Bearer token authentication for API requests; no OAuth flow was verified in gathered evidence.
raw_result.gating_reason: Pricing page shows a Standard plan that is free up to the first 15K credits with a self-serve 'Get started' flow.

api_surface.breadth: Public developer docs include quickstart, API reference, parse endpoints, rate limits, concurrency throttle, CLI, and MCP server documentation, indicating a reasonably broad REST API for document parsing workflows.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official documentation sidebar includes a 'Reducto MCP Server' page, indicating an existing MCP offering.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Gathered evidence indicates documented rate limits around 1000 requests/second sustained for most endpoints, with a lower limit around 200 requests/second for polling endpoints; pricing page also mentions '200 guaranteed concurrent pages' on the Standard tier.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs expose quickstart, API reference, rate limits, CLI, and MCP server pages; pricing is transparently self-serve and usage-based; documentation also appears to mention webhooks and exponential backoff guidance.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Some fields rely on browser-run memory rather than directly quoted final extracts: exact auth label (Bearer token vs API key classification), exact scope of REST breadth, and whether MCP availability is fully public/open-source versus only documented. No explicit official GitHub repository URL was verified from the gathered evidence.

result.description: Document ingestion and parsing API for turning complex PDFs, images, and spreadsheets into structured data for AI workflows.
result.auth_notes: Official docs indicate Bearer token authentication for API requests; no OAuth flow was verified in gathered evidence.
result.gating_reason: Pricing page shows a Standard plan that is free up to the first 15K credits with a self-serve 'Get started' flow.

api_surface.breadth: Public developer docs include quickstart, API reference, parse endpoints, rate limits, concurrency throttle, CLI, and MCP server documentation, indicating a reasonably broad REST API for document parsing workflows.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official documentation sidebar includes a 'Reducto MCP Server' page, indicating an existing MCP offering.

result.rate_limit_notes: Gathered evidence indicates documented rate limits around 1000 requests/second sustained for most endpoints, with a lower limit around 200 requests/second for polling endpoints; pricing page also mentions '200 guaranteed concurrent pages' on the Standard tier.
result.notable_signals: Official docs expose quickstart, API reference, rate limits, CLI, and MCP server pages; pricing is transparently self-serve and usage-based; documentation also appears to mention webhooks and exponential backoff guidance.
result.human_review_reason: Some fields rely on browser-run memory rather than directly quoted final extracts: exact auth label (Bearer token vs API key classification), exact scope of REST breadth, and whether MCP availability is fully public/open-source versus only documented.
